channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/diegosouzapw — Never stop coding. Free MIT AI gateway: one endpoint, 268+ providers (50+ free), 500+ models — Claude, GPT, Gemini, Kimi K3, GLM, DeepSeek. Works with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Cline & Copilot. Quota-aware auto-fallback, RTK+Caveman compression saves 15-95% tokens, MCP/A2A, multimodal, Desktop/PWA. Built by 500+ contributors.
item.description: Open-source MIT AI gateway providing single endpoint access to 268+ providers and 500+ models including Claude, GPT, and Gemini.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/msitarzewski — A complete AI agency at your fingertips - From frontend wizards to Reddit community ninjas, from whimsy injectors to reality checkers. Each agent is a specialized expert with personality, processes, and proven deliverables.
item.description: Specialized AI agents with distinct roles and personalities collaborate as a complete digital agency.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/topoteretes — Cognee is the open-source AI memory platform for agents. Give your AI agents persistent long-term memory across sessions with a self-hosted knowledge graph engine.
item.description: Cognee is an open-source AI memory platform enabling agents to maintain persistent long-term memory using self-hosted knowledge graphs.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] iptv-org/iptv — Collection of publicly available IPTV channels from all over the world
item.description: GitHub repository collects publicly available IPTV channels from around the world.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] handy-computer/transcribe.cpp — ggml speech-to-text inference for 16+ model families
item.description: New transcribe.cpp tool enables ggml-based speech-to-text inference for 16+ model families.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] moonshine-ai/moonshine — Very low latency speech to text, intent recognition, and text to speech, for building voice agents and interfaces
item.description: Moonshine enables low-latency speech-to-text, intent recognition, and text-to-speech for voice agent development.

item.title: [Hacker News] Exploit brokers pay $500k for WordPress RCEs. I found one with GPT5.6 and $25
item.description: Exploit brokers pay $500k for WordPress RCEs. I found one with GPT5.6 and $25

item.title: [Hacker News] How proprietary formats have become Microsoft’s main tool for lock-in
item.description: How proprietary formats have become Microsoft’s main tool for lock-in

item.title: [Hacker News] LoRA Speedrun – a public wall-clock leaderboard for fine-tuning techniques
item.description: LoRA Speedrun – a public wall-clock leaderboard for fine-tuning techniques

item.title: [Hacker News] Power companies are using eminent domain to seize land for data centers
item.description: Power companies are using eminent domain to seize land for data centers

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Large Language Models as Unified Multimodal Learners for Clinical Prediction
item.description: Language models fine-tuned on serialized clinical data match or exceed specialized multimodal systems across clinical prediction tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Verbalizable Representations Form a Global Workspace in Language Models
item.description: Interpretability technique identifies verbalizable representations in language models that function as a global workspace, exposing internal reasoning and deliberation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] VarRate: Training-Free Variable-Rate KV Cache Compression for Long-Context LLMs
item.description: VarRate, a training-free method, improves KV cache compression by assigning variable-rank budgets to tokens based on salience, avoiding accuracy loss.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] EpiNarrate: Agentic Generation of Grounded Narratives from Epidemiological Scenario Projections
item.description: EpiNarrate framework generates consistent public health narratives from epidemiological projections using structured reasoning and maximum-entropy selection.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] SkillCorpus: Consolidating and Evaluating the Open Skill Ecosystem for Real-World LLM Agents
item.description: SkillCorpus consolidates 96,401 LLM agent skills from 821,000 sources and improves agent task performance by up to 7.5 percentage points.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Process Reward Informed Tree Rollout for Effective Multi-Turn RL
item.description: PATR, a new reinforcement learning method for LLM agents, uses process feedback to guide tree-based rollouts, improving performance on FrozenLake and SWE-Bench.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] On the Structure of Address in Multi-Party Dialogue: From Discrete Labels to Continuous Levels
item.description: Researchers model addressee detection in multi-party dialogue as continuous rather than discrete, showing continuous representations better predict turn-taking and listener behaviors.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Adaptive Multi-Step Lookahead Decoding for Diffusion Language Models
item.description: AdaLook framework adaptively selects lookahead depth in masked diffusion language model decoding, achieving better accuracy-efficiency trade-offs than fixed-depth approaches.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Better Starts, Better Ends: Bootstrapped Iterative Self-Reasoning Distillation for Compressed Reasoning
item.description: BIRD reduces reasoning model compute via two-stage self-distillation: filtering concise solutions before on-policy reverse-KL training.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Before the Action: Benchmarking LLMs on Prospective Hypothesis Discovery
item.description: Researchers introduce HypoArena benchmark to evaluate LLMs' ability to generate hypotheses from inconclusive evidence across six scientific domains.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Cost-efficient generative AI summarization for scalable automated essay scoring in educational assessment
item.description: Study shows GPT-5 variants can summarize long essays for automated scoring, with trade-offs between model size, summarization quality, and computational cost.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] CAMMAR: Culture-Aware Matryoshka for Metaphorical Arabic Representations
item.description: CAMMAR representation learning framework separates lexical, cultural, and metaphorical information in Arabic embeddings through nested subspaces.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Contextual Semantic Relevance Tracks fMRI BOLD Responses During Naturalistic Speech Comprehension
item.description: Contextual semantic relevance, not word surprisal, better predicts brain fMRI responses during naturalistic speech comprehension.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Conditional Reliability of Toxicity Signals for Multilingual and Code-Mixed Abuse Detection
item.description: ToxGate conditions external toxicity signals on context to improve abuse detection in multilingual and code-mixed text.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] An MLIR-Based Compilation Method for Large Language Models
item.description: Researchers present an MLIR-based compilation method for deploying large language models on specialized hardware using framework-independent and hardware-specific operator dialects.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] How Much Human Label Variation Does Formal Semantic Structure Explain?: Group-Level Effects and Item-Level Ceilings in NLI
item.description: Formal semantic structure explains only 3-3.6 percent of human label variation in natural language inference.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] DECODEM: Data Extraction from Corporate Organizational Documents via Enhanced Methods
item.description: DECODEM is a benchmark dataset enabling LLM-based extraction of corporate governance variables from documents with high accuracy but variable performance across provisions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Induction in Both Directions: A Mechanistic Analysis of In-Context Learning in Masked Diffusion Language Models
item.description: Diffusion language models implement bidirectional induction circuits for in-context learning, outperforming autoregressive models when both sides of context are visible.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] From Plausible to Actionable: A Position on LLM Self-Explanations
item.description: Paper argues LLM self-explanations appear plausible but lack proven faithfulness to actual reasoning, though remain actionable for decision-support.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] BayesPO: Bayesian Prompt Optimization via Parallel-Tempered Gradient-Guided Discrete MCMC
item.description: BayesPO applies Bayesian posterior sampling via gradient-guided MCMC to prompt optimization, improving instruction-induction accuracy from 60% to 63%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Candidate Attended Dialogue State Tracking Using BERT
item.description: BERT-based framework enables zero-shot multi-domain dialogue state tracking without additional training.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Loop the Loopies!
item.description: Loopie looped Transformer series outperforms vanilla baselines with equivalent compute and achieves gold-medal performance at IMO and IPhO.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Frontier AI performance across the business disciplines: a case-grounded benchmark of knowledge work and analytical reasoning
item.description: New BusinessCaseBench benchmark shows frontier LLMs perform well on complex business analytical reasoning tasks drawn from business school case studies.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Frontier Language Models Struggle to Copy: Text Can Be Better Viewed in 2D
item.description: Frontier LLMs fail at copying text due to 1D positional encoding limitations; 2D-RoPE addresses this by viewing text in 2D grids.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Controlling Implicit Shortcut Reliance in L2 Spoken English Auto-markers
item.description: Training method reduces classifier reliance on exploitable shortcuts in automated language proficiency assessment systems.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Rate-Utility Frontiers for Language Encodings: Comparing Tokens, Bytes, and Pixels Under Controlled Linguistic Content
item.description: Study compares tokens, bytes, and pixels as text encodings, finding each performs best for different tasks and computational constraints.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ToolSciVer: Multimodal Scientific Claim Verification with Visual Tool Augmented Reinforcement Learning
item.description: ToolSciVer enhances multimodal scientific claim verification by equipping vision models with type-aware visual tools for interpreting figures, tables, and charts in research papers.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Empathy as Predictive Misalignment Tolerance: A Co-Regulation Framework and the Regime Structure of Dialogue Repair
item.description: Researchers propose reframing empathy in dialogue systems as maintaining predictive misalignment tolerance rather than emotional resonance, revealing noise-dependent repair trade-offs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Hidden in Thought: Transferable Chain-of-Thought Artifacts Induce Harmful Behavior
item.description: Harmful chain-of-thought reasoning from compromised language models can transfer to other models and be distilled into effective jailbreak attacks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Looped Latent Attention: Cross-Loop KV Compression for Looped Transformers
item.description: Looped Latent Attention codec compresses recurrent K/V caches in weight-tied Transformers by storing low-rank latents, achieving up to 21.3x compression.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Structure of the Circular-Dyadic Convolution Error
item.description: Substituting Hadamard transform for DFT in circular convolution introduces structured, predictable algebraic error governed by filter alignment and positioning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Position: Quantum Program Generation Must Prioritize Validity Over Probabilistic Scaling
item.description: Quantum circuit generation requires verification-aware architectures encoding task-specific rules, not just scaling, because valid programs decay exponentially with more qubits.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Transportable Threshold-Based Framework for Interpretable Classification of Medical Data
item.description: Interpretable clinical classification framework using Bernoulli Naive Bayes with statistical binarization achieves performance comparable to complex models on benchmark datasets.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Regularity-Aware Stochastic MGDA with Adaptive Conflict-Avoidant Update Direction Control
item.description: Stochastic multi-objective learning method improves convergence rate from T^-1/4 to T^-1/2 by exploiting regularity conditions in conflict-avoidant gradient directions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] AI Trading: Evaluating Large Language Models for Technical Market Analysis
item.description: Study evaluates five LLMs for technical market analysis; GPT-4 Turbo and FinGPT outperform S&P 500 but exhibit persistent failure modes.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] qZACH-ViT: Quantization-Aware Intrinsic Explanations with Recursive Attribution-Stabilized Optimization
item.description: Researchers develop qZACH-ViT, a quantized medical image classifier achieving 99.975% prediction agreement with the original model while maintaining interpretability through RASO optimization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] From hyperplanes to hyperellipsoids: characterizing the inherent interpretability of linear and single-qubit mixed-state binary classification models
item.description: Research shows single-qubit mixed-state models classify via hyperellipsoids rather than hyperplanes, clarifying quantum machine learning's relationship to classical linear models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Stochastic Reset Pathfinding: Path-Level Regret for Cascading Bandits over Graph Paths
item.description: Stochastic Reset Pathfinding introduces path-level regret analysis for learning routes in graphs with unreliable edges across quantum networks and mesh systems.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Who Became Financially Vulnerable After COVID-19? A Population-Level Machine Learning Analysis Using MEPS Data
item.description: Machine learning models identified poverty, insurance coverage, and prescription spending as strong predictors of healthcare financial vulnerability before and after COVID-19.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] LLM4EHR: Aligning Clinical Time Series with Medical Event Sequences via Large Language Models
item.description: Clinical foundation model LLM4EHR aligns electronic health record events with time series using LLMs and transformers for improved downstream clinical predictions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Relevant and Irrelevant: A Renormalization Group Analysis of Transformer Attention
item.description: Renormalization group analysis shows transformer attention is relevant for long-range correlations but irrelevant for short-range, with first-layer heads dominating.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Looped Latent Attention: Cross-Loop KV Compression for Looped Transformers
item.description: Looped Latent Attention compresses key-value caches in weight-tied Transformers by exploiting low-rank structure across loops, achieving up to 21.3x compression.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Robust Peak-cost Constrained Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Robust peak-cost constrained RL framework maximizes expected reward while ensuring trajectory safety under simulator-to-reality dynamics mismatches.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] ADS-C: Antidistillation Sampling for Classification
item.description: ADS-C defends classifiers against knowledge distillation attacks by perturbing output distributions while maintaining top-1 prediction accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Deep Learning Approaches for Sleep Apnea Classification from Polysomnographic EEG Signals
item.description: Vision Transformer models on EEG signals achieve 0.75 AUC for pediatric sleep apnea detection, enabling automated screening with identified clinical deployment challenges.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Inpainting Insights: Elevating Visual XAI with Photorealistic Perturbations
item.description: Study improves LIME's image explanations using generative inpainting to create photorealistic perturbations that better align with data distribution.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Diffusion models recover accurate mixture weights despite score function insensitivity
item.description: Diffusion models accurately recover mixture weights in multimodal distributions despite target score insensitivity to weight changes.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] An Auto-Scaling Approach for Serverless Environments Based on a Multi-Expert Consensus Mechanism
item.description: Framework for serverless autoscaling uses dependency graphs, ensemble forecasting models, and cost optimization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Cache-Aware Prompt Compression:A Two-Tier Cost Model for LLM API Caching
item.description: Research proposes Cache-Aware Prompt Compression combining query-agnostic compression with cache control, achieving 49-64% cost reduction over existing LLM caching strategies.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Recursive Harness Self-Improvement
item.description: RHI iteratively refines task-specific agent harnesses using feedback, improving low-reasoning-effort agent performance while reducing inference costs by up to 60%.
